framework: ec-cube
version: "4.x"
last_updated: "2026-04-28"

status: official
---

# EC-CUBE 4.x — Tech Pack

EC-CUBE is Japan's dominant open-source e-commerce platform. Built on Symfony with Twig templating. If a Japanese store is not Shopify or WooCommerce, it is almost certainly EC-CUBE. Version 4.x is the current line; 2.x and 3.x are legacy and use a completely different architecture (Smarty templates, custom MVC — not Symfony).

Symfony version by EC-CUBE release: 4.0 → Symfony 3.4, 4.1 → Symfony 4.4, 4.2 → Symfony 5.4, 4.3 → Symfony 6.4 + PHP 8.3.

## 1. Fingerprinting Signals

| Signal | Type | Value | Confidence |
|--------|------|-------|------------|
| `eccube` session cookie | Cookie | name `eccube` (default, configurable via `ECCUBE_COOKIE_NAME`) | High |
| `eccube_remember_me` cookie | Cookie | set when customer checks "remember me" at login | High |
| `eccube_2fa` cookie | Cookie | set when 2FA is enabled (`ECCUBE_2FA_COOKIE_NAME`) | Medium |
| `/html/template/` in asset URLs | HTML substring | `src="/html/template/default/assets/` | High |
| `/html/user_data/` in HTML | HTML substring | user-uploaded assets at `/html/user_data/assets/` | High |
| `/html/plugin/` in HTML | HTML substring | plugin-owned public assets | Medium |
| `Twig` template comments | HTML comment | `{# … #}` comment blocks in rendered source (dev only) | Medium |
| Symfony `X-Debug-Token` header | Response header | present in dev/profiler mode; absent in production | Dev only |
| Symfony `X-Debug-Token-Link` header | Response header | links to `/_profiler/{token}`; never in production | Dev only |
| Wappalyzer result | MCP | `"EC-CUBE"` | Definitive |
| Japanese locale + JPY + Symfony stack | Combined signal | `ECCUBE_LOCALE=ja`, `ECCUBE_CURRENCY=JPY`, Twig templates | High |
| `/_profiler/` or `/_wdt/` accessible | URL probe | Symfony web profiler — should be blocked in production | Dev only |
| `X-Powered-By: PHP/...` response header | Response header | EC-CUBE 4.x is PHP; Symfony does not suppress this by default | Low |
| `eccube.yaml` / `eccube_nav.yaml` path leak | Source/error | config paths visible in stack traces | High |

**Version extraction:**
```bash
# EC-CUBE 4.x does NOT emit a generator meta tag by default.
# Version is embedded in composer.json (not web-accessible) and GitHub releases.
# Best proxy: identify Symfony version from X-Powered-By or profiler, then cross-reference:
#   EC-CUBE 4.0 → Symfony 3.4
#   EC-CUBE 4.1 → Symfony 4.4
#   EC-CUBE 4.2 → Symfony 5.4
#   EC-CUBE 4.3 → Symfony 6.4 + PHP 8.3

# Check for Symfony version hint in headers (dev only):
curl -sI {site} | grep -i "x-powered-by\|x-debug-token"

# Check sitemap to narrow down: EC-CUBE 4.1+ includes sitemap.xml by default
curl -sI {site}/sitemap.xml
# 200 → likely 4.1+; 404 → likely 4.0 or sitemap plugin not installed

# Check for 2FA cookie presence (4.2+ feature):
# ECCUBE_2FA_ENABLED=true is the default in 4.2+
```

## 2. Default API Surfaces

### Front-office routes (always active, no auth required)

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|-------|
| `/` | GET | None | Homepage (`TopController`) |
| `/products/list` | GET | None | Product listing; supports query params `?category_id=`, `?name=`, `?orderby=`, `?pageno=` |
| `/products/detail/{id}` | GET | None | Product detail page (`id` = integer) |
| `/products/add_cart/{id}` | POST | None | Add to cart; returns JSON `{"done": bool, "messages": [...]}` when `X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest` |
| `/products/add_favorite/{id}` | GET/POST | Session (customer) | Add to wishlist/favorites |
| `/cart` | GET | None | Cart page |
| `/cart/buystep/{cart_key}` | GET | None | Initiate checkout for cart key |
| `/shopping` | GET | Session (customer) | Checkout page |
| `/shopping/confirm` | POST | Session (customer) | Confirm order |
| `/shopping/checkout` | POST | Session (customer) | Place order |
| `/shopping/complete` | GET | Session (customer) | Order complete |
| `/mypage/login` | GET/POST | None | Customer login |
| `/mypage/` | GET | Session (customer) | Customer account dashboard |
| `/mypage/history/{order_no}` | GET | Session (customer) | Order history detail |
| `/mypage/favorite` | GET | Session (customer) | Customer favorites list |
| `/sitemap.xml` | GET | None | Sitemap index (EC-CUBE 4.1+); links to sub-sitemaps |
| `/sitemap_product.xml` | GET | None | Product detail page sitemap |
| `/sitemap_category.xml` | GET | None | Product category/listing sitemap |
| `/sitemap_page.xml` | GET | None | Static pages sitemap |

### Admin routes (always active, auth required)

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|-------|
| `/{admin_route}/` | GET | Admin session | Admin dashboard; default route is `admin` |
| `/{admin_route}/login` | GET/POST | None | Admin login form |
| `/{admin_route}/product` | GET | Admin session | Product list |
| `/{admin_route}/order` | GET | Admin session | Order list |
| `/{admin_route}/customer` | GET | Admin session | Customer list |

### Web API plugin routes (requires `eccube-api4` plugin installation)

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|-------|
| `/api` | GET/POST | OAuth2 Bearer token | GraphQL endpoint — single endpoint for all queries and mutations |
| `/{admin_route}/authorize` | GET/POST | Admin session | OAuth2 Authorization Code endpoint |
| `/token` | POST | Client credentials | OAuth2 token endpoint |
| `/{admin_route}/api/config` | GET | Admin session | API plugin admin config page |
| `/{admin_route}/api/oauth` | GET | Admin session | OAuth2 client management |

## 3. Config / Constants Locations

| Location | What's there | How to access |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `.env` / `.env.dist` | All runtime config: DB URL, admin route, cookie name, locale, currency, 2FA settings | Source only; never web-accessible |
| `app/config/eccube/packages/eccube.yaml` | EC-CUBE parameter defaults (`eccube_admin_route`, `eccube_user_data_route`, etc.) | Source only |
| `app/config/eccube/packages/framework.yaml` | Symfony session config — cookie name, lifetime, path; asset base paths (`/html/template/%eccube.theme%`) | Source only |
| `app/config/eccube/packages/security.yaml` | Firewall definitions: `admin` firewall (`/%eccube_admin_route%/`), `customer` firewall | Source only |
| `app/config/eccube/routes.yaml` | Route loading — all controllers discovered by annotation | Source only |
| `html/template/default/assets/` | Front-end CSS/JS assets (web-accessible) | Public GET |
| `html/user_data/` | User-uploaded content, custom CSS (`html/user_data/assets/css/customize.css`) | Public GET |
| `/sitemap.xml` | Site URL structure; reveals product IDs and category slugs | Public GET |

**Key environment variables (from `.env.dist`):**

| Variable | Default | Effect |
|----------|---------|--------|
| `ECCUBE_ADMIN_ROUTE` | `admin` | Admin panel URL segment — frequently changed in production |
| `ECCUBE_USER_DATA_ROUTE` | `user_data` | User-data URL segment |
| `ECCUBE_COOKIE_NAME` | `eccube` | Session cookie name |
| `ECCUBE_2FA_COOKIE_NAME` | `eccube_2fa` | Two-factor auth cookie name |
| `ECCUBE_LOCALE` | `ja` | Site locale |
| `ECCUBE_CURRENCY` | `JPY` | Currency |
| `ECCUBE_TEMPLATE_CODE` | `default` | Active theme |
| `APP_ENV` | `prod` | `dev` enables profiler, debug headers |
| `APP_DEBUG` | `0` | `1` enables Symfony debug mode |

## 4. Auth Patterns

| Pattern | Location | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| Session cookie `eccube` | `Cookie: eccube=<session-id>` | Single Symfony session cookie for both front and admin; name is configurable |
| Admin CSRF token | Hidden `_token` field in admin forms | Symfony form CSRF; valid per-form, not per-session |
| Customer "remember me" | `eccube_remember_me` cookie | Set on customer login with "remember me" checked; lifetime 3600s default |
| OAuth2 Bearer token | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` | For API plugin; Authorization Code flow only (client_credentials and password disabled by default) |
| OAuth2 scopes | `read`, `write` | Default scope is `read`; `write` required for mutations |
| Admin two-factor auth | `eccube_2fa` cookie | Set after 2FA verification; expires per `ECCUBE_2FA_EXPIRE` (default 14 days) |

**OAuth2 Authorization Code flow:**
```
1. GET /{admin_route}/authorize?response_type=code&client_id={id}&redirect_uri={uri}&scope=read+write
2. Admin logs in → approves → redirect with ?code={auth_code}
3. POST /token  Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
   grant_type=authorization_code&code={auth_code}&client_id={id}&client_secret={secret}&redirect_uri={uri}
   → {"access_token": "...", "token_type": "Bearer", "expires_in": 3600, "scope": "read write"}
4. POST /api  Authorization: Bearer {access_token}  Content-Type: application/json
   {"query": "{ products { id name } }"}
```

## 5. JS Bundle Patterns

| Path | Content |
|------|---------|
| `/html/template/default/assets/js/bundle.js` | Main front-end JS bundle (webpack) — includes jQuery and third-party libs |
| `/html/template/default/assets/js/eccube.js` | EC-CUBE core front-end logic |
| `/html/template/default/assets/js/function.js` | Utility functions |
| `/html/template/admin/assets/js/bundle.js` | Admin panel JS bundle |
| `/html/template/{theme}/assets/js/bundle.js` | Active theme JS (theme name from `ECCUBE_TEMPLATE_CODE`) |
| `/html/user_data/assets/js/*.js` | Custom JS added by site operator |
| `/html/plugin/{PluginCode}/assets/js/*.js` | Plugin-owned public JS |

Check each bundle URL + `.map` suffix for source maps.

## 6. Source Map Patterns

EC-CUBE 4.x ships with CSS source maps in the default template:
- `/html/template/default/assets/css/style.css.map` — source map for `style.css` (present in default install)
- `/html/template/default/assets/css/style.min.css.map` — source map for minified CSS

Sass source files at `html/template/default/assets/scss/` are NOT web-accessible (no webroot exposure of `.scss` files).

JS bundles are webpack-compiled. Source maps for JS are NOT included by default in production builds. Custom themes built with source map output enabled may expose `.js.map` files. Check: `{bundle-url}.map` — if 200, recover original source paths.

Admin template assets follow the same pattern under `/html/template/admin/assets/`.

## 7. Common Plugins & Extensions

| Plugin | Code | Detection signal | Notes |
|--------|------|-----------------|-------|
| Web API (GraphQL) | `Api42` | `/api` endpoint returns GraphQL response; OAuth client management at `/{admin}/api/oauth` | Official plugin; not bundled |
| Coupon | `Coupon4` | `/html/plugin/Coupon4/` in assets; coupon form on cart page | Official; very common |
| Mail Magazine | `MailMagazine4` | Unsubscribe links in emails; admin route `/{admin}/mail_magazine/` | Official; widely deployed |
| Recommend | `Recommend4` | Recommended products block in HTML; `/html/plugin/Recommend4/` | Official |
| Sales Report | `SalesReport4` | Admin-only; no front-end signals | Official |
| Payment (various) | Varies | Payment plugin assets at `/html/plugin/{PluginCode}/`; checkout page loads plugin JS | 3rd-party; common providers include GMO, SoftBank, Stripe |
| Sitemap XML Generator | `SitemapXml` | `/sitemap.xml` with plugin-specific structure (EC-CUBE 4.0 before built-in sitemap) | Plugin for 4.0; built-in from 4.1 |
| Security Check | `SecurityChecker` | Admin-only; no front-end signals | Official security plugin |

**Plugin detection via HTML:**
```bash
# Plugin assets are served from /html/plugin/{PluginCode}/
curl -s {site} | grep -oP '/html/plugin/\K[^/"]+' | sort -u
# Returns plugin codes present on the page
```

## 8. Known Public Data

| Endpoint | Data | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| `/products/list` | Product list with names, prices, images, category info | HTML page; paginated; supports `?name=`, `?category_id=`, `?orderby=`, `?pageno=` |
| `/products/list?orderby=1` | Products ordered by price (ascending) | `orderby=1` price asc, `orderby=2` price desc, `orderby=3` newest |
| `/products/detail/{id}` | Full product detail: price, stock, description, variants, images | Integer IDs; sequential from 1 |
| `/sitemap.xml` | Sitemap index listing all sub-sitemaps | Present by default in EC-CUBE 4.1+ |
| `/sitemap_product.xml` | All product detail URLs with `<lastmod>` | Enumerates entire product catalog |
| `/sitemap_category.xml` | All product listing/category URLs | Reveals category structure |
| `/sitemap_page.xml` | Static page URLs | May reveal custom pages |
| `/html/user_data/assets/css/customize.css` | Custom CSS applied by site operator | May reveal business-specific branding |
| `/api` (POST GraphQL, if API plugin active) | Products, orders, members per OAuth2 scope | Requires Bearer token with appropriate scope |

**GraphQL query examples (API plugin):**
```graphql
# Product list
{ products { id name ProductClasses { id code price02 stock } Status { id name } } }

# Order list (requires write scope or admin token)
{ orders { id order_no name01 name02 Shippings { postal_code Pref { name } addr01 addr02 } } }

# Filter orders by date
{ orders(order_date_start: "2024-01-01") { id order_no name01 name02 } }
```

## 9. Probe Checklist

Run these in order. Record result (✓ 200 / ✗ 403 / – 404) for each.

- [ ] `GET /` — Homepage; confirms EC-CUBE is active; check for `/html/template/` in source
- [ ] `GET /products/list` — Product listing; confirms front-office is functional
- [ ] `GET /products/detail/1` — First product; confirms sequential integer IDs
- [ ] `GET /cart` — Cart page; check for `eccube` session cookie being set
- [ ] `GET /mypage/login` — Customer login; CSRF form token visible in source
- [ ] `GET /sitemap.xml` — Sitemap index (200 = EC-CUBE 4.1+; 404 = 4.0 or plugin needed)
- [ ] `GET /sitemap_product.xml` — Full product catalog enumeration
- [ ] `GET /sitemap_category.xml` — Category structure
- [ ] `GET /admin/` — Default admin route; expect redirect to `/admin/login` if not authenticated
- [ ] `GET /html/template/default/assets/css/style.css` — Confirms asset path structure
- [ ] `GET /html/template/default/assets/css/style.css.map` — CSS source map (commonly present)
- [ ] `GET /html/template/default/assets/js/bundle.js` — JS bundle
- [ ] `GET /html/user_data/assets/css/customize.css` — Custom CSS (may be empty or absent)
- [ ] `POST /api` with `{"query": "{ products { id name } }"}` — GraphQL endpoint (200 = API plugin installed; 404 = not installed)
- [ ] `GET /_profiler/` — Symfony profiler (should be 404 in production; 200 = dev mode, major info leak)
- [ ] `GET /_wdt/` — Symfony web debug toolbar (should be 404 in production)
- [ ] `GET /html/plugin/` — Plugin asset directory listing (usually 403; directory names reveal installed plugins)
- [ ] Check response cookies on `GET /` — Look for `eccube`, `eccube_2fa`, `eccube_remember_me`

## 10. Gotchas

- **Admin route is almost always renamed in production.** The default is `/admin` but EC-CUBE prompts operators to set a custom path during installation (`ECCUBE_ADMIN_ROUTE`). Do not assume `/admin` will resolve — enumerate with common names or check `app/config/eccube/packages/eccube.yaml` if you have source access.

- **Single session cookie for front and admin.** EC-CUBE 4 does NOT use separate `eccube_front` and `eccube_admin` cookies (as is sometimes documented for EC-CUBE 2.x/3.x). There is one session cookie named `eccube` (configurable). The admin and customer sessions are separated by Symfony's security firewall configuration, not by separate cookie names.

- **GraphQL API is NOT bundled.** The `eccube-api4` plugin must be explicitly installed and activated. A 404 on `/api` simply means the plugin is absent, not that the site is locked down. Many production sites do not install it.

- **OAuth2 Authorization Code flow is the only supported grant by default.** The API plugin disables `client_credentials`, `password`, and `implicit` grants in its default `services.yaml`. Do not assume other grant types work without verifying the plugin configuration.

- **EC-CUBE 4.x vs 2.x/3.x are completely different platforms.** Signals from older versions (Smarty templates, `data/Smarty/` paths, `html/user_data/packages/` paths, `eccube_front`/`eccube_admin` as separate cookies) do NOT apply to 4.x. If you see Smarty template paths, you are on EC-CUBE 2.x/3.x.

- **Symfony profiler in production is a critical vulnerability.** If `/_profiler/` or `/_wdt/` return 200, the site is in dev mode. The profiler exposes full request/response details, POST parameters, and session data — including admin credentials submitted to login forms. Report immediately.

- **`/html/` is the document root, but many hosting setups change this.** The `html/` directory is EC-CUBE's webroot. On shared hosting or some server configs, the server's document root may be set directly to `html/`, making asset paths start at `/template/default/` instead of `/html/template/default/`. If `/html/template/` returns 404 but `/template/` works, the webroot is already inside `html/`.

- **CSS source maps are present by default.** Unlike WordPress, EC-CUBE ships `style.css.map` and `style.min.css.map` in its default template. These are not accidentally leaked — they are committed to the repository. However, they do reveal the Sass source file structure and any path information embedded in the map.

- **CSRF tokens are per-form, not per-session.** EC-CUBE uses Symfony's CSRF protection with a hidden `_token` field on all state-changing forms. Tokens are scoped to the form type (e.g., `add_cart`, `shopping_confirm`), not universally reusable.

- **The `eccube_2fa` cookie is checked separately from the session.** When 2FA is enabled (`ECCUBE_2FA_ENABLED=true`, the default in 4.2+), admin login requires a time-based OTP. The `eccube_2fa` cookie records the device as verified for `ECCUBE_2FA_EXPIRE` days (default 14). Absence of this cookie on admin login causes 2FA to be required again.

## 11. GitHub Code Search Patterns

Use these queries on GitHub to find custom endpoints, plugin code, and configuration examples for this framework.

### Framework-Specific Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `"<pattern>" language:<lang> path:<path>` | <description> |

### Example Queries

```bash
# Search for custom endpoints
site:github.com "<framework>" "api" filetype:<ext>

# Search for auth patterns  
site:github.com "<framework>" "auth" "middleware"

# Search for config files
site:github.com "<framework>" "config" "endpoint"
```

## 12. Framework-Specific Google Dorks

### Discovery Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `site:{domain} inurl:<path>` | <description> |

### Complete Dork List

```
# API endpoints
site:{domain} inurl:/api/
site:{domain} inurl:/v1/

# Framework paths
site:{domain} inurl:<specific-path>
```


## 13. Cross-Cutting OSINT Patterns

These patterns apply across frameworks and should be checked for any detected technology.

### Favicon Hashing

Identify technology stack by hashing favicon and searching Shodan/Censys for same stack:

```bash
# Get favicon hash (mmh3 hash of favicon content)
curl -s "https://{domain}/favicon.ico" | python3 -c "
import sys, hashlib
data = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
# Simple mmh3 hash simulation using Python
try:
    import mmh3
    print('Favicon hash:', mmh3.hash(data))
except ImportError:
    print('Install mmh3: pip install mmh3')
"

# Search Shodan for same favicon (indicates shadow IT subdomains)
# site:shodan.io search: icon_hash:{hash}
```

**What it reveals:** Hidden subdomains running same framework stack as main site.

### Source Map Discovery

Check for source maps across all JS bundles:

```bash
# Extract all JS bundle URLs from HTML
curl -s "https://{domain}/" | grep -oP 'src="[^"]+\.js[^"]*"' | grep -oP '"[^"]+"' | tr -d '"' > js_urls.txt

# Check each for .map file
while read url; do
  map_url="${url}.map"
  status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${map_url}")
  [ "$status" = "200" ] && echo "SOURCE MAP: ${map_url}"
done < js_urls.txt
```

**Build tool patterns:**
| Build Tool | Source Map Pattern | Detection |
|------------|-------------------|------------|
| Webpack | `{bundle}.js.map` or `//# sourceMappingURL=` | Check response header `X-SourceMap` |
| Vite | `{name}-[hash].js.map` | Vite manifest `manifest.json` |
| Rollup | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| esbuild | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| Next.js | `/_next/static/chunks/*.js.map` | Only if `productionBrowserSourceMaps: true` |

### Tech Stack → API Pattern Mapping

Auto-map detected frameworks to likely endpoint patterns:

| Framework | Common API Patterns |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Next.js | `/api/*`, `/_next/data/*`, `/api/auth/*`, `/api/trpc/*` |
| WordPress | `/wp-json/*`, `/wp-json/wp/v2/*`, `/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` |
| Shopify | `/api/2024-10/graphql.json`, `/products.json`, `/collections.json` |
| Rails | `/api/v1/*`, `/assets/*`, `/users/sign_in` |
| Laravel | `/api/*`, `/livewire/message/*`, `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` |
| Strapi | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/api/upload*` |
| Magento | `/rest/V1/*`, `/pub/static/*` |
| Django | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/accounts/*` |
| Express | `/api/*`, `/v1/*`, `/health` |
| Astro | `/_astro/*`, `/api/*` |
| Ghost | `/ghost/api/*`, `/members/api/*` |

When Phase 3 detects a framework, use this table to prioritize Phase 5/6/7 probes.

### Email Naming Convention Analysis

Extract emails from theHarvester/GitHub results to predict internal subdomains:

```bash
# Sample emails found: john.doe@example.com, jane.smith@example.com
# Predicted subdomains: mail.example.com, smtp.example.com, exchange.example.com

# Common patterns:
# first.last@ → internal.example.com, mail.example.com
# firstinitial+last@ → owa.example.com, outlook.example.com
```

**Add to Phase 9 session brief:** Note email patterns and predicted subdomains.
